The first thing you should know about all that is if you are niched somehow, it is infinitely easier than if you are not niched. And usually the tighter niche that you are, the better. Then it is usually more a financing problem than it is a marketing problem. For example, I can tell you, Charlie Martin's case from zero to, what did you say? What do you got? How many people you got in coach? Huh? 28. 28, what did they pay up front? 28. Okay, so 28 of them at $25,000 plus, what are they paying a month? 12.97. Okay, plus 12.97 a month, okay. Now she skips all the steps, okay. So there is no product, there is no newsletter, there is no herd, there is no host relationship, there is no nothing. That is Charlie going to dentistry, selling them into coaching, $25,000, $12,000 a month. Time is actually the same thing in chiropractic with franchise, on and on and on. I could give you 20 examples. And the thing we know from all of them is that cost of sales is going to be somewhere between $5,000 and $9,000. So literally, if you tell me you want 100 of them, and you'll give me the budget of $5,000 to $9,000 times 100, and you're narrow enough niched, and you got a story, it's formulaic. So this is more like a financing problem than it is a marketing problem. The more niched you are, the tighter the niche you are, so he's not just going to, he's not like even going after dentists, he's going after high end cosmetic or implant dentists, and there's other criteria as well. So if you're going to get clients cold, here's the things you got to think about. One is create a hit list and farm them. So when David Ogilvy went in the advertising business, he said, I'm going to make a list of 100 companies that we want as clients, and we're just going to pursue them again and again and again and again and again forever until we get them. And ultimately got like 92 or 93, I forget the exact story. Well, most people never even do that. They don't even sit down and say, okay, here's 100 people I want, here's the 10 people I want, here's the 50 people I want. If there were 20 businesses in this niche that I really want more than any of the other 20, right? So somehow you got to create your hit list. And if the list is too big, odds are you're not going to get where you want to go. Because very few people, most people are way too broad, you know, and they describe to me who their prospects are. Their definition is so broad that the resources you would have to have to have any impact, to do an effective job, you would have to put so many marbles on a table, you wouldn't be bothering to do it. You'd just be clipping the coupons from your marbles. So you got to narrow the field so a reasonable pot of resources can be used to have impact. So most people are way too broad. And so you got to build your hit list, your segment of the segment of the segment of the market, who you are then going to farm. By far it's a real estate sales term. It essentially in real estate where it came from, what they originally meant was you pick a tiny little subdivision, you call out your farm and everybody in there gets mailings from you. You go doorknock, you're at the PTA meeting, you're out there on Halloween giving away pumpkins, you're driving around with your magnetic signs on your car every day, up and down those streets, you really work that farm. And real estate agents who are good at it, it's a perfectly viable technique today, I teach it, it's in magnetic marketing. You know, you'll drive through a neighborhood and you'll only see one realtor sign, you'll hardly ever see anybody else's. That means she's doing a good job. She owns that territory. Well, you got to take the same approach. You got to carve out a territory that's manageable to you and it may be spread all over the country. By territory I may mean it may be 500 names on a list. But you got to carve out a territory and then you got to like be ever present. So you got to be far known. So you got to be there, be there, be there, be there, be there. That may or may not mean physically. So for many years one of my farms was NSA. I've been using that as an example now today. So I mean I farmed it. I mean I mailed the hell out of it. I advertised in the publications. I went to every event. I made sure I was visible at every event. So I did all the manual labor stuff. I contributed to the foundations and the charities. I went and spoke at the local chapters. I was ever present.
